name: Visual Interference
summary: Visual design choices that hide, obscure, or make it hard to perceive information the user needs.

evidence.claim: Visual interference is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy.

body.description: Critical information — total price, cancellation options, data-sharing details — rendered in tiny font, low-contrast color, or hidden behind expandable sections. Buttons for business-preferred actions made visually prominent while user-preferred actions are minimized (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: Run axe-core-style accessibility checks on the page; flag disclosure text failing AA contrast or under 12px.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given the page's visual hierarchy, identify any disclosure text or user-preferred-action controls rendered in ways that reduce perceptibility (low contrast, small size, collapsed sections, off-screen). Return {violations: [{element, issue}]}.
body.remediation: Surface the hidden disclosure in plain view. Summarize its content for the user.
